tip: Always use dependency injection in your Spring applications to promote loose coupling and enhance testability.
Spring Boot's auto-configuration automatically configures Spring application based on jar dependencies on the classpath. Use @SpringBootApplication to enable it.
Design your REST APIs with proper HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and meaningful status codes for better client communication.
Leverage Spring Data JPA repositories to reduce boilerplate code. Extend JpaRepository for automatic CRUD operations and custom query methods.
Use @ExceptionHandler and @ControllerAdvice in Spring to centralize exception handling and provide consistent error responses.
Understand Spring bean scopes: Singleton (default), Prototype, Request, Session, and Application. Choose the right scope for your use case.
Use AOP with Spring to implement cross-cutting concerns like logging, security, and transactions without cluttering your business logic.
Implement authentication and authorization using Spring Security. Use SecurityFilterChain to configure your security settings.
Use @SpringBootTest and @MockBean for integration testing. Mock external dependencies to keep your tests fast and isolated.
Enable Spring Boot Actuator to expose operational endpoints for health checks, metrics, and application monitoring in production.
